untranslatable_reason: This is a stop-loss discipline rule based on MAE/ATR analysis, not a position sizing rule. It describes WHEN to place stops and HOW to calculate stop thresholds dynamically, but does not specify HOW MUCH capital to risk per trade or how to scale position size. Position size would be determined separately based on the stop distance (risk per unit) and a chosen sizing method (e.g., 1R, fixed fractional). This is a stop-placement mechanism, not sizing allocation.
